high_value.name: impersonate
high_value.why: Direct account takeover primitive if auth check is missing or guessable.
abuse_ideas: Call without auth and with a low userId to see if it returns an AuthPayload.
Call as low-priv user against userId of an admin and check returned token claims.

high_value.name: setRole
high_value.why: Privilege-escalation primitive — a missing @auth directive turns this into vertical priv-esc.
abuse_ideas: Authenticate as a non-admin and call setRole(userId: <self>, role: ADMIN).
Probe Role enum via __type(name: "Role") for hidden values like SUPERADMIN.

high_value.name: user
high_value.why: ID-keyed lookup is a classic IDOR target if the resolver does not enforce ownership.
abuse_ideas: Iterate sequential ID values and diff response shapes for cross-tenant leakage.
Request sensitive fields like email/role on other users' IDs.

high_value.name: adminUsers
high_value.why: Admin-named query that takes no args — easy auth-check regression target.
abuse_ideas: Query as anonymous and as low-priv user; any non-empty result is a finding.

high_value.name: uploadAvatar
high_value.why: Upload scalar plus account-bound side effect — test for SSRF, path traversal, content-type confusion.
abuse_ideas: Upload SVG with embedded JS to test for stored XSS on profile rendering.
Send oversized file and an HTML/JS file to test extension/MIME validation.

auth_signals: No @auth/@admin directive present in introspection — relying on resolver-side checks only.
next_steps: Run each mutation unauthenticated to map which require auth vs which silently allow it.
Diff field-level responses on user(id:) between owner and stranger sessions.
